title: Julius Malema: From rally gunfire to court
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"NATIONAL NEWS - EFF leader Julius Malema’s problem in relation to today’s sentencing started on 28 July, 2018 after he fired a gun in the air during a rally in Mantashe, East London, Eastern Cape.","html":"NATIONAL NEWS - EFF leader Julius Malema’s problem in relation to today’s sentencing started on 28 July, 2018 after he fired a gun in the air during a rally in Mantashe, East London, Eastern Cape."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"When confronted by the authorities, he claimed that the rifle that he used when firing several times in the air was a toy gun that belonged to a director of a security company, Adrian Snyman."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Malema’s reckless act was filmed by his supporters and the same year, AfriForum and other bodies took legal action.","html":"Malema’s reckless act was filmed by his supporters and the same year, AfriForum and other bodies took legal action."},{"type":"heading","text":"Trial proceedings and verdict","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In 2019, the trial began, whereby 19 witnesses testified against Malema and his bodyguard, Snyman.","html":"In 2019, the trial began, whereby 19 witnesses testified against Malema and his bodyguard, Snyman."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Snyman was acquitted but Malema was found guilty of charges including the discharge of a firearm in public, possession of an unlicensed firearm and ammunition.","html":"Snyman was acquitted but Malema was found guilty of charges including the discharge of a firearm in public, possession of an unlicensed firearm and ammunition."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"After Malema was found guilty, the case was postponed to today for a pre-sentencing hearing.","html":"After Malema was found guilty, the case was postponed to today for a pre-sentencing hearing."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Experts suggest that Malema will appeal the judgment.","html":"Experts suggest that Malema will appeal the judgment."},{"type":"heading","text":"AfriForum welcomes verdict","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Jacques Broodryk, AfriForum’s chief spokesperson for community safety, said justice has finally been served – despite years of delays and irregularities in this case.","html":"Jacques Broodryk, AfriForum’s chief spokesperson for community safety, said justice has finally been served – despite years of delays and irregularities in this case."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“The evidence overwhelmingly proved that Malema committed several serious offences under the Firearms Control Act."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The verdict confirms that those who think they are untouchable will eventually be held accountable, no matter how long it takes,” Broodryk said.","html":"“The verdict confirms that those who think they are untouchable will eventually be held accountable, no matter how long it takes,” Broodryk said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"He said his organisation played a crucial role in bringing this case to trial.","html":"He said his organisation played a crucial role in bringing this case to trial."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Without the organisation’s involvement, the case may never have seen the inside of a courtroom, Broodryk said.","html":"Without the organisation’s involvement, the case may never have seen the inside of a courtroom, Broodryk said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen","html":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Julius Malema: From rally gunfire to court
